Manis just put an AI agent on your desktop and it changes everything. So Manis just dropped a desktop AI agent that works directly on your computer kind of like Claude Cowork and it just changed what's possible for you. So this is available right now. And here's what you need to understand. For years AI [music] tools lived in the browser. You typed, you answered, you copied and pasted. That era is over. Manage my computer which just dropped means a real AI agent now sits on your desktop. It can open your files, run your terminal, control your apps, um build apps on your machine, for example. It can ask permission before it does anything as well so that you stay in control. And it's super easy and easy to use. So, this is a digital employer that works on your computer. built in manos does That's the difference is you get access to a lot more APIs whereas with claude co-work for example if you're using that locally unless you give it an API key which is a lot of work and then you have to manage all your APIs you're not going to have access to this stuff so it's a lot more powerful and it just works off it's already completed the task for organizing our folders and That's where we're at right now. So, it's super easy. The other thing I would say here is like you might compare it to something like for example open claw but bear in mind like this just works straight out the box. If you're using something like open claw it breaks a lot. It gets confused. It struggles with different things. Even when you update it disconnects quite often and so if you're using something like manus computer it's a lot more powerful. I think the main thing with all these tools though is like you just want to pick one and stick with it because switching between different tools you got different login different apps connected to each one that gets kind of confusing to manage. So, here's how to think about Anna, my computer, right? Imagine hiring a very fast assistant and this assistant can sit at your computer whilst you're in a meeting or at the gym or out on a walk and whilst you're gone, it's quietly working through the list you gave it. Sorting files, renaming things, building scripts, running code, pulling information from your local tools. This is what Manis my computer basically does, right? And there's four different layers to this. So, you've got like your local machine access. So, Manis desktop runs on your Mac or Windows machine. It connects directly to your file system. It means it can read, edit, move and organize real files on computer and this is a brand new update, right? Whereas for example um with other tools you just don't get this. The other thing you get is you stay in control. So every single terminal command requires your approval before it runs it. And you get two choices. You can allow once and approve it just one time for that one task as a one-off or you can click on always allow and it takes that permission and uses it every single time. Right? other way you're in charge and manus isn't going to touch anything that you've not said yes to which is important because a lot of people are worried like these AI agents locally on the files are just going to go off and do some crazy stuff now in the meantime we're like okay well I need a a new thumbnail for my YouTube video about openclaw we could just paste in that command like you can see here and that's just going off and doing it and we can say okay openclaw is an AI agent open source do a quick Google then create the thumbnail keep it minimal plus attention grabbing right [snorts] and it will just go off and start doing this right so it will search for information about that topic and then it will go off and create the actual image itself so you can see here it's just going off visiting the website this AI agent could browse the web for you directly and you could have as many uh many different tasks as you wanted running in the side panel you can see for example each day I actually schedule tasks like you see right here. And what this allows me to do is search for AI news updates daily. And so I can organize and just search for that latest AI news every single day, like you can see right here. And it will just go off and search the web and and give us a a daily news brief like you can see. It even creates that inside a a document as you can see right here. And we could even turn that into a website later. But basically, you now get the full power of Manus, but you get it with something that connects to your local files as well, which is super powerful right here. Now the final one is layer number four which is recurring routines and schedule tasks. This is where it gets really powerful. So manus integrates with projects, agents and schedule tasks. What that means is you can set up recurring local automations. So for example, you can tidy your downloads folder every single morning. You can say okay do that every single morning. Here's an example of that working. Right? So if we go back to the chat where we've organized our local files, we can say right, okay, do this every morning plus schedule it in. And then we can say, okay, 6:00 a.m. daily PST, right? Something like that. Now, in the meantime, we've got the actual thumbnail generated over here.
